successCriteria.description: src/shared/types/leave.types.ts defines LeaveType string enum with exactly three values: ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY — each member explicitly assigned its string value
src/shared/types/leave.types.ts defines LeaveStatus string enum with exactly four values: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED — each member explicitly assigned its string value

tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts contains Jest unit tests that verify every LeaveType value (ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY) and every LeaveStatus value (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED) is defined, and that each enum has the correct cardinality (3 and 4 respectively)
No files outside the two specified paths (src/shared/types/leave.types.ts and tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts) are created or modified in this phase

constraints: LeaveType and LeaveStatus must be TypeScript string enums (not const enums, not union types, not plain objects) — each member explicitly assigned its string value (e.g., ANNUAL = 'ANNUAL')
The file must be at the exact path src/shared/types/leave.types.ts — this path is referenced by every downstream phase in PLAN.md and ARCHITECTURE.md
The test file must be at tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts and must use Jest (the declared test framework in HARNESS.json stack)
No other files are created or modified — this phase is scoped to exactly two files
TypeScript strict mode compliance: no implicit any, strict null checks (per AGENTS.md and HARNESS.json constraints.rules no-any)

Any database schema, migrations, repository implementations, service logic, controllers, routes, or validation schemas — this phase is purely type definitions

ambiguities.description: The intent says "Create src/shared/types/leave.types.ts" but the file already exists on disk with the exact content specified. This could indicate the phase was already executed. The intent-agent rules instruct us to treat the canonical specification as authoritative — the file must exist with the specified content regardless of whether it's a create or verify operation.
options: Treat as create-or-verify: ensure the file exists with the exact enum definitions specified
Treat as create-only: fail if the file already exists
Treat as no-op: skip since the file already matches
